author: @Dudelee1
text:
Even though i didn’t know you personally we did have some email correspondence over an invention idea, it meant the world to me that you got back to me with suggestions and that you liked my idea. I’ll sorely miss just knowing you were there tinkering away still.

RIP Robert

author: @levon9999
text: Yellow flame is very dirty. Any way yo make it burn better by adding multiple spiral coils around the top and enhancing it with additional air holes in potentially multiple layers of rings added as well? Ive seen backyard leaf burners burning with blue clean flame.

author: @BroversXproductions
text: it's a sooty flame because of its high carbon content. perhaps something that could be done to improve, say, the efficiency of biodiesel is to hydrogenate the lipids and dissolve them in an NPS during the synthesis, thus increasing the hydrogen/carbon ratio leading to more complete combustion.

author: @brianmurphy8790
text:
If air was rushing out lots of 0.5-1mm holes, in the inner can - just above the wick, it will act as a flame spreader.
Put a hole in the bottom of each and fasten them together with at least a 22mm brass fitting. That will allow air to get in and rush out the holes in the inner can.
Obviously, you'd need a top and bottom of the inner can - so ya gotta make one from two.
Lose the fill hole too, because you don't want air coming out of it.

replies.author: @TrumpstienPhile
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering wonder if the smoke could be decomposed with a lid made of platinum  coated ceramic(from old vehicle catalytic converter- catalytic  converters get red/white hot when waste gasses are forced through  them(similar to  gas soldering  iron).

Also I vaguely  recall  survivalists/ off grid types passing high voltage  through  a spark gap  submerged  in oil which generated a flammable gas- I think they called it 'bingo gas'- not sure of the efficiency tho
